mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in unemployment rate below 10% signals improved labor market conditions, boosting corporate earnings visibility and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in 60%+ of instances where UNRATE declines below 10%.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in industrial production above 100.5 index points indicates expanding manufacturing activity, generating positive momentum in S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are negative in 50%+ of cases where INDPRO exceeds 100.5 over 20-day horizons.

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent rise in housing starts (HOUST) and real sales (RSAFS) signals strong consumer demand, creating positive momentum in S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint effect of HOUST and RSAFS fails to explain 30%+ of SPX return variation over 60-day horizons.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI reading above 260 signals persistent inflationary pressures, prompting central bank hawkishness and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are positive in 60%+ of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 260 over 20-day horizons.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL indicate tightening monetary policy amid inflation, creating headwinds for equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction between FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL fails to explain 40%+ of SPX return variation over 5-day horizons.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, rising FEDFUNDS reduces corporate borrowing capacity, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are positive in 50%+ of instances during FEDFUNDS increases over 20-day horizons.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly GDP growth above $17.2 trillion signals robust economic expansion, generating positive momentum in S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are negative in 50%+ of cases where GDPC1 exceeds $17.2 trillion over 60-day horizons.
